title: 10 best herbal capsules for excessive sweating
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for excessive sweating are dietary supplements that contain natural ingredients, such as sage, chamomile, and schisandra, which have been traditionally used to regulate perspiration. These herbs help reduce excessive sweating by supporting the nervous system, promoting healthy digestion, and regulating hormonal balance. For example, sage has antihydrotic properties that can help control sweat gland activity, while chamomile and schisandra have calming effects that can help reduce stress-induced sweating. By taking herbal capsules, individuals can experience a significant improvement in their quality of life, as they no longer have to worry about embarrassing sweat stains or the discomfort of constantly feeling damp. Moreover, herbal capsules provide a natural and safe alternative to prescription medications, which can have unpleasant side effects.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood capsule helps with excessive sweating because it contains artemisinin, a compound with antiperspirant and antimicrobial properties. This substance helps regulate sweat production, reduces odor caused by bacterial breakdown of sweat, and soothes irritated skin. Moreover, wormwood has anti-inflammatory properties that alleviate discomfort and inflammation associated with excessive sweating. By addressing the root causes of excessive sweating, wormwood capsules promote a balanced and healthy perspiration process, enhancing overall well-being and self-confidence.
remedy_properties: Anticholinergic property: Wormwood capsules contain artemisinin and flavonoids that help block the action of acetylcholine, a neurotransmitter that stimulates sweat glands, thereby reducing excessive sweating.
Astringent property: The tannins present in wormwood have an astringent effect on the skin, which helps constrict sweat glands and control excessive perspiration.
Antimicrobial property: Wormwood has antimicrobial properties due to the presence of artemisinin and camphene, which help prevent body odor caused by the bacterial breakdown of sweat.
Anti-inflammatory property: Wormwood contains sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory effects, helping to soothe irritated skin and reduce inflammation caused by excessive sweating.
Anxiolytic property: Wormwood has been shown to have anxiolytic effects due to the presence of α-pinene, which may help alleviate anxiety-induced sweating by promoting relaxation and reducing stress.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the wormwood plant contain compounds that can help regulate sweat production, making them ideal for medicinal capsules.
Flowers: Wormwood flowers have drying properties, which can help reduce excessive sweating when taken in capsule form.
Seeds: Wormwood seeds are rich in essential oils, which have a calming effect on the body and can help reduce sweating.
Bark: The bark of the wormwood plant contains tannins, which have astringent properties that can help control sweating.
remedy_recipe: Purchase dried wormwood herb and size "00" empty vegetarian capsules, along with a capsule-filling machine.
Measure out 500mg of dried wormwood herb for each capsule, ensuring to fill 20-25 capsules for a one-month supply.
Grind the dried wormwood herb into a fine powder using a coffee or spice grinder, then pour the powder into the capsule-filling machine.
Following the machine's instructions, fill each capsule with the wormwood powder, tapping the machine gently to ensure a snug fit.
Store the filled capsules in a cool, dry place, taking one capsule up to three times daily for excessive sweating.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Belladonna capsule helps with excessive sweating because it contains alkaloids that can help regulate the functioning of the glands responsible for sweat production. Belladonna, also known as deadly nightshade, has anticholinergic properties that can block the action of acetylcholine, a neurotransmitter that activates sweat glands. By inhibiting the activity of these glands, belladonna capsules can help reduce excessive sweating, promoting a more comfortable and confident feeling for individuals experiencing this condition. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new supplement regimen.
remedy_properties: Anticholinergic: Belladonna capsules contain anticholinergic alkaloids (such as atropine, hyoscyamine, and scopolamine) that help block the action of acetylcholine, a neurotransmitter involved in the activation of sweat glands, thereby reducing excessive sweating.
Astringent: Belladonna contains tannins that have an astringent effect, which can help constrict sweat glands and reduce perspiration.
Analgesic: Belladonna has analgesic properties due to the presence of alkaloids like hyoscyamine and scopolamine, which can help alleviate any discomfort or pain associated with excessive sweating.
Anti-inflammatory: Belladonna has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the sweat glands and skin, thereby minimizing excessive sweating.
Sedative: Belladonna contains sedative alkaloids (such as hyoscyamine and scopolamine) that can help calm the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress-induced excessive sweating.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the belladonna plant contain alkaloids that can help regulate the production of sweat.
Flowers: Belladonna flowers have anticholinergic properties, which can be useful in reducing excessive sweating.
Roots: The roots of the plant contain the highest concentration of alkaloids, making them a potent ingredient for medicinal capsules.
Seeds: Belladonna seeds are rich in scopolamine, an alkaloid that can help control the activity of the sweat glands.
Bark: The bark of the belladonna plant contains hyoscyamine, an alkaloid that can help reduce excessive sweating.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 cup of dried belladonna leaves and thoroughly clean them to remove any impurities.
Grind the belladonna leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle, then pass the powder through a mesh sieve to ensure uniform consistency.

Measure out 500mg of the belladonna powder and combine it with an excipient, such as microcrystalline cellulose, in a 1:2 ratio (e.g., 500mg of belladonna powder to 1000mg of microcrystalline cellulose) to improve the flow properties of the mixture.
Fill size "0" gelatin or vegetarian capsules with the belladonna-excipient mixture using a capsule filling machine or by hand.
Store the completed capsules in a cool, dry place, away from direct sunlight, and use within six months of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Daisy capsule helps with excessive sweating because it contains natural compounds that regulate the body's sweat glands. The herb has been used traditionally to treat various skin conditions, including excessive perspiration. Daisy extract is known to have astringent properties that tighten and constrict the skin's pores, reducing the amount of sweat that is released. Additionally, daisy has anti-inflammatory and soothing effects that can alleviate any irritation or discomfort caused by excessive sweating. By regulating sweat production and soothing the skin, daisy capsules can help individuals manage excessive sweating and improve their overall quality of life.
remedy_properties: Astringent properties: Daisy capsules contain tannins that help to tighten pores and reduce sweat production, making it useful in managing excessive sweating.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The presence of flavonoids in daisy capsules helps to reduce inflammation and soothe skin irritation caused by excessive sweating.
Antiperspirant properties: Daisy capsules contain sesquiterpene lactones that inhibit the activity of sweat glands, providing a natural alternative to chemical antiperspirants.
Antibacterial properties: The antibacterial properties of daisy capsules, due to the presence of essential oils, help to prevent body odor caused by excessive sweating.
Diaphoretic properties: Daisy capsules contain volatile oils that promote perspiration, helping to eliminate toxins from the body while regulating body temperature and reducing excessive sweating.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the daisy plant are used to make medicinal capsules for excessive sweating due to their astringent properties which help to constrict sweat glands.
Leaves: Daisy leaves are often used in medicinal capsules for excessive sweating as they contain tannins that can help to reduce perspiration.
Flowers: The flowers of the daisy plant are used in medicinal capsules for excessive sweating because they contain flavonoids that have a calming effect on the body, reducing overall perspiration.
Buds: Daisy buds are used in medicinal capsules for excessive sweating due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce redness and irritation caused by excessive sweating.
Seeds: Daisy seeds are used in medicinal capsules for excessive sweating as they contain essential oils that can help to regulate the body's natural perspiration process.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh daisy flowers, rinse, and dry thoroughly, allowing 24 hours for optimal moisture reduction.
Grind dried daisy flowers into a fine powder, using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle, for 5-10 minutes.
Measure 2 teaspoons of daisy powder and mix with 1 teaspoon of binding agent (such as tapioca starch or arrowroot powder) for consistent capsule filling.
Fill size "00" empty gelatin or vegetarian capsules with the daisy-binding agent mixture, using a capsule filling machine or by hand, for efficient and accurate dosing.
Store the completed daisy capsules in an airtight container, in a cool, dark place, for up to 6 months, ensuring potency and freshness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold capsule helps with excessive sweating because it contains anti-inflammatory and sweat-regulating properties. The herb, also known as Calendula officinalis, has been used traditionally to treat various skin conditions, including excessive sweating. It works by constricting the sweat glands, reducing the production of sweat, and providing relief from wetness and discomfort. Moreover, pot marigold has antimicrobial and astringent properties that help maintain skin health and prevent irritation caused by excessive sweating. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and irritation caused by excessive sweating, thanks to its active constituents like flavonoids and carotenoids.
Astringent: Its astringent properties help tighten skin pores and reduce excessive secretions, including sweat, due to the presence of tannins.
Antimicrobial: Pot marigold's antimicrobial properties protect the skin from infections caused by sweat and bacteria, as it contains active compounds like lutein and zeaxanthin.
Diaphoretic: It helps regulate body temperature and sweat production by promoting perspiration, owing to its active constituents like quercetin and rutin.

Vulnerary: Pot marigold's vulnerary properties aid in wound healing and skin regeneration, reducing the chances of skin irritation and inflammation caused by excessive sweating, attributed to its active compounds like triterpenes and saponins.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Pot marigold flowers are used in medicinal capsules for excessive sweating due to their antiperspirant and astringent properties.
Leaves: The leaves of the pot marigold plant are used in medicinal capsules for excessive sweating because they contain tannins and flavonoids that help to reduce sweating.
Stems: Pot marigold stems are used in medicinal capsules for excessive sweating as they contain compounds that help to constrict sweat glands and reduce perspiration.

Seeds: The seeds of the pot marigold plant are used in medicinal capsules for excessive sweating because they contain essential oils that have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce sweating.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 grams of dried pot marigold flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground pot marigold with 1 gram of filler such as microcrystalline cellulose or powdered rice flour.
Using a capsule filling machine or by hand, fill size "0" empty gelatin capsules with the herbal mixture, making approximately 20-25 capsules.
Store the completed capsules in a cool, dry, and dark place, such as an airtight container, to preserve their potency.
Take 1-2 capsules, as needed, up to three times a day, 30 minutes before meals, with a full glass of water. Consult a healthcare professional before starting any new supplement regimen.

Flowers: Chicory flowers are sometimes used in medicinal capsules for excessive sweating due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe irritated skin and reduce redness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Coptis chinensis capsule helps with excessive sweating because it contains high levels of berberine, a powerful alkaloid that has been shown to regulate sweat gland activity. This herbal supplement has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help reduce any skin irritation or infection that may result from excessive sweating. Moreover, Coptis chinensis has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries to treat various health conditions, including damp-heat syndrome, which is often associated with excessive sweating. By balancing the body's internal environment and reducing inflammation, Coptis chinensis capsule can effectively manage excessive sweating and improve overall health.
remedy_properties: Anticholinergic effect: This property helps reduce excessive sweating by inhibiting the activity of the parasympathetic nervous system, which is responsible for activating the sweat glands. The active constituents responsible for this property include berberine, coptisine, and palmatine.

Astringent effect: Coptis chinensis capsules have an astringent effect that helps constrict body tissues, including sweat glands, thereby reducing the production of sweat. The tannins present in the plant give it this astringent effect.

Anti-inflammatory effect: Excessive sweating can lead to skin irritation and inflammation. The anti-inflammatory effect of Coptis chinensis helps alleviate these symptoms, providing relief to individuals experiencing excessive sweating. The active constituents responsible for this property include berberine and palmatine.

Antibacterial effect: Sweaty areas of the body are prone to bacterial infections. The antibacterial effect of Coptis chinensis helps prevent and treat such infections, promoting overall skin health. The active constituents responsible for this property include berberine, coptisine, and palmatine.

Sedative effect: Excessive sweating can be a symptom of anxiety and stress. The sedative effect of Coptis chinensis helps alleviate anxiety and stress, reducing the occurrence of excessive sweating. The active constituents responsible for this property include berberine and palmatine.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain high levels of alkaloids, which help regulate the production of sweat and reduce excessive sweating.
Rhizomes: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, helping control sweat gland activity and minimize sweating.
Flowers: Contain essential oils and flavonoids that have a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing stress-induced sweating.
Seeds: Packed with berberine, an alkaloid that helps balance the body's autonomic nervous system, reducing excessive sweating.
remedy_recipe: Purchase dried Coptis chinensis root, typically containing 6-8% alkaloids, and a vegetarian capsule-making machine.
Grind 20 grams of dried Coptis chinensis root into a fine powder, using a high-speed grinder for 3-5 minutes.
Load the vegetarian capsules with 300-400mg of the Coptis chinensis root powder per capsule, using the capsule-making machine.
Seal the capsules and store in a cool, dry place, ensuring the capsules are protected from light.
Take 2-3 capsules daily, 30 minutes before meals, for a period of 1-2 months, or as directed by a healthcare professional.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horsetail capsule helps with excessive sweating because it contains high levels of silica, a mineral that is known to help strengthen connective tissues, such as those found in the skin. By promoting the health and integrity of the skin, horsetail capsules can help regulate sweat gland function and reduce excessive sweating. Additionally, horsetail is a natural diuretic, which can help eliminate excess fluid from the body and further reduce sweating. Overall, horsetail capsules offer a natural and effective solution for managing excessive sweating.
remedy_properties: Astringent: Horsetail's astringent properties help to tighten pores and reduce sweat production, thus decreasing excessive sweating. This is due to the presence of tannins and silica in horsetail.
Diuretic: Horsetail helps eliminate excess fluid from the body, reducing overall wetness and sweating. The active constituent responsible for this property is equisetonin.
Antiperspirant: Horsetail has antiperspirant properties that can help to directly reduce sweating. This is due to the presence of silica and other organic compounds in horsetail.

Anti-inflammatory: Horsetail has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the sweat glands, reducing excessive sweating. The active constituents responsible for this property are flavonoids and phenolic acids.
Nervine: Horsetail has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce stress-induced sweating. This is due to the presence of alkaloids and saponins in horsetail.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: Horsetail rhizomes are rich in silica and have astringent properties, making them useful for excessive sweating by helping to tighten pores and reduce fluid loss.
Stems: Horsetail stems contain high levels of potassium and antioxidants, which can help regulate electrolyte balance and reduce sweating by supporting overall body health.
Leaves: Horsetail leaves have diuretic properties, which can help reduce sweating by promoting fluid excretion through urination instead of perspiration.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried horsetail herb and grind it into a fine powder, which may take 5-10 minutes.
Mix the horsetail powder with 1 teaspoon of tapioca starch or another natural binder, ensuring thorough combination.
Gradually add just enough water to create a dough-like consistency, kneading for 2-3 minutes.
Form the dough into small capsule-shaped pieces, then dry them in a dehydrator or at room temperature for 12-24 hours.
Fill size 00 vegetarian capsules with the dried horsetail pieces, using a capsule filling machine or manually.

remedy_parts: Seeds: Fennel seeds are commonly used in making medicinal capsules for excessive sweating due to their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help regulate sweat glands.
Leaves: The leaves of the fennel plant are beneficial in managing excessive sweating, as they contain tannins that can help constrict sweat glands and reduce perspiration.

Stems: Fennel stems are used in medicinal capsules for excessive sweating because they contain essential oils and flavonoids, which can have a calming effect on the nervous system and reduce sweating.
Flowers: Fennel flowers are a good source of antioxidants and can help regulate sweat gland activity, making them a useful addition to medicinal capsules for excessive sweating.

remedy_recipe: Gather and finely grind 2 tablespoons of dried fennel seeds, setting aside 30 minutes for this task.
Mix the ground fennel seeds with 1 teaspoon of tapioca starch, thoroughly blending the ingredients for 5 minutes.
Fill size "00" empty gelatin capsules with the fennel-tapioca mixture, allotting 20 minutes for this step.
Allow the filled capsules to dry for 1 hour to ensure proper sealing.
Store the herbal fennel capsules in an airtight container for up to 6 months, taking 1-2 capsules as needed to help control excessive sweating.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsule helps with excessive sweating because it contains an active compound called glycyrrhizin, which inhibits the production of cortisol, a stress hormone that can trigger sweat glands to produce more sweat. Licorice also has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to excessive sweating. Additionally, licorice has been shown to have a balancing effect on the autonomic nervous system, which can help regulate bodily functions, including sweat production. By taking licorice capsules, individuals may be able to reduce their overall sweat production and experience a decrease in excessive sweating.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Licorice contains glycyrrhizin, which helps reduce inflammation and irritation in the sweat glands, providing relief from excessive sweating.
Adrenal support: Licorice helps regulate cortisol levels, reducing the body's stress response, which can lead to decreased sweating.
Antimicrobial: Licorice has antimicrobial properties, which help prevent the growth of bacteria and fungi on the skin, reducing the risk of skin infections and body odor caused by excessive sweating.
Diuretic: Licorice promotes urination, helping the body eliminate excess fluids and reducing overall sweating.
Hypotensive: Licorice can help lower blood pressure, reducing the body's overall sympathetic nervous system activity, which can help reduce excessive sweating.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain triterpenoid saponins, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory effects and can help reduce sweating.
Seeds: The seeds of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizin, which has been used in traditional medicine to treat a variety of conditions, including excessive sweating.
Bark: The bark of the licorice plant contains flavonoids and coumarins, which have been shown to have a sedative effect and can help reduce stress-induced sweating.
Leaves: The leaves of the licorice plant contain isoliquiritigenin, which has been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, and can help regulate sweat gland function.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2000mg of dried licorice root and a capsule filling machine.
Grind the licorice root into a fine powder, using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Load the powdered licorice root into the capsule filling machine, and compress it into gelatin capsules.
Ensure each capsule contains 250mg of licorice root powder.
Store the completed licorice root capsules in a cool, dry place, and take one to two capsules daily as needed for excessive sweating.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Witch hazel capsule helps with excessive sweating because it contains tannins, which have astringent properties that can help to tighten and constrict pores, reducing the amount of sweat that is able to reach the surface of the skin. Additionally, witch hazel has anti-inflammatory and styptic properties, which can help to soothe irritated skin and reduce redness and swelling. This can be particularly beneficial for people who experience excessive sweating, as it can help to alleviate the discomfort and skin irritation that often accompanies this condition. Furthermore, witch hazel has been shown to have antibacterial and antifungal properties, which can help to prevent the growth of odor-causing bacteria and fungi on the skin, helping to keep sweat odor under control.
remedy_properties: Astringent: Witch hazel extract contains tannins that have an astringent effect, helping to tighten pores and reduce excessive sweating.

Anti-inflammatory: The presence of gallic acid and other compounds in witch hazel provides anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate irritation and inflammation caused by excessive sweating.
Antioxidant: Witch hazel contains potent antioxidants, such as proanthocyanidins and flavonoids, which can help protect skin cells from damage caused by excessive sweating and maintain skin health.
Antimicrobial: Witch hazel has antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent bacterial growth and reduce the risk of skin infections associated with excessive sweating.
Vasoconstrictor: Witch hazel constituents, such as o-glycosides and essential oils, can help constrict blood vessels and reduce blood flow to the skin, potentially decreasing sweating and redness.
remedy_parts: Bark: The bark of the witch hazel plant is often used in medicinal capsules for excessive sweating due to its astringent properties, which can help to reduce sweat production.
Leaves: The leaves of the witch hazel plant are commonly used in medicinal capsules for excessive sweating because they contain tannins, which can help to constrict pores and reduce sweat release.

Twigs: The twigs of the witch hazel plant are sometimes used in medicinal capsules for excessive sweating due to their high concentration of polyphenols, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help to regulate sweat gland activity.

Flowers: The flowers of the witch hazel plant are occasionally used in medicinal capsules for excessive sweating due to their soothing and calming effects, which can help to reduce anxiety and stress-related sweating.

Seeds: The seeds of the witch hazel plant are very rarely used in medicinal capsules for excessive sweating, but they do contain oils that have been shown to have astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce sweat production and irritation.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried witch hazel leaves and 1 teaspoon of baking soda, then mix them together in a small bowl.
Add 1 cup of boiling water to the mixture, let it steep for 15-20 minutes, then strain and allow it to cool.
Mix 2 teaspoons of cornstarch with a small amount of cold water to form a paste.
Combine the cooled witch hazel mixture and cornstarch paste in a small saucepan, heat over low heat while stirring until it thickens.
Once cooled, fill size 00 gelatin capsules with the mixture and store in a cool, dry place.
